curriculum.subject: Chemistry
curriculum.overallDescription: 10 units, 9 chapters, and 0 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Express the concentration of solutions using various units
Describe vapour pressure of liquid solutions
Distinguish between ideal and non-ideal solutions
Apply colligative properties to determine molar mass
Explain the working of electrochemical and galvanic cells
Apply the Nernst equation in cell potential calculations
Describe processes in electrolytic cells and electrolysis
Differentiate between batteries, fuel cells, and corrosion mechanisms
Define the rate of a chemical reaction

Characteristic tests of carbohydrates, fats and proteins in pure samples and their detection in given foodstuffs
Determination of concentration/molarity of KMnO4 solution by titrating against Oxalic acid or Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate
Qualitative analysis: Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt
practicals.details: Qualitative Analysis (Section B Mandatory). Cations include Pb, Cu, Al, Fe, Mn, Ni, Zn, Co, Ca, Sr, Ba, Mg. Anions include CO3, S, SO3, NO3, NO2, Cl, Br, I, SO4, PO4, CH3COO.

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

formative_topics: Surface Chemistry - Adsorption (physisorption and chemisorption, factors affecting adsorption of gases on solids, colloidal state distinction between true solutions, colloids and suspension; lyophilic, lyophobic properties of colloids; coagulation, emulsion)
General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Elements
Polymers – Polymerisation, Homopolymers and copolymer with few examples

Chemistry in Everyday life - Chemicals in medicines (analgesics, tranquilizers antiseptics, disinfectants, antimicrobials, antifertility drugs, antibiotics, antacids, antihistamines) and food (preservatives, artificial sweetening agents, antioxidants)
internal_assessment.note: Topics included for formative assessment only to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments.

written_test_details.type: Practical skill based very short answer type questions

mandatory_experiments_section_b.title: Quantitative analysis
experiments: (a) Preparation of a given volume of the standard solution of Oxalic acid. (b) Determination of molarity of KMnO4 solution by titrating it against a standard solution of Oxalic acid.
Conducting exercise F1 using Ferrous ammonium sulphate (Mohr's salt).

mandatory_experiments_section_b.title: Qualitative Analysis
mandatory_experiments_section_b.description: Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt.

optional_experiments_section_a.title: Surface Chemistry
experiments: Preparation of one lyophilic and one lyophobic sol

optional_experiments_section_a.title: Chromatography
optional_experiments_section_a.description: Separation of pigments from extracts of leaves and flowers by paper chromatography and determination of Rf values.

optional_experiments_section_a.title: Characteristic tests of carbohydrates and proteins
Preparation of Inorganic Compounds - Potash Alum

Study of the effect of Potassium Bisulphate as food preservative under various conditions (temperature, concentration, time).
Study of digestion of starch by salivary amylase and effect of pH and temperature on it.
Comparative study of the rate of fermentation of wheat flour, gram flour, potato juice, carrot juice, etc.
Extraction of essential oils present in Saunf (aniseed), Ajwain (carom), Illaichi (cardamom).
Study of common food adulterants in fat, oil, butter, sugar, turmeric powder, chili powder and pepper.

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

competency_groups.title: Solutions
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to types, concentration expressions, solubility, vapor pressure, ideal/non-ideal solutions, and colligative properties.
competencies: Identify different types of solutions
Express the concentration of solutions using various units
Explain factors affecting solubility
Describe vapour pressure of liquid solutions
Distinguish between ideal and non-ideal solutions
Apply colligative properties to determine molar mass

competency_groups.title: Electrochemistry
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to electrochemical cells, Nernst equation, conductance, electrolysis, batteries, fuel cells, and corrosion.
competencies: Explain the working of electrochemical and galvanic cells
Apply the Nernst equation in cell potential calculations
Analyze conductance of electrolytic solutions
Describe processes in electrolytic cells and electrolysis
Differentiate between batteries, fuel cells, and corrosion mechanisms

competency_groups.title: Chemical Kinetics
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to reaction rates, factors influencing rate, integrated rate equations, temperature dependence, and collision theory.
competencies: Define the rate of a chemical reaction
Analyze factors influencing the rate of reaction
Derive and apply integrated rate equations for zero and first-order reactions
Explain the temperature dependence of reaction rates using Arrhenius equation concepts
Describe collision theory of chemical reactions

competency_groups.title: d and f Block Elements
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to periodic table position, electronic configuration, properties of transition elements, lanthanoids, actinoids, and applications.

Write electronic configurations for d-block elements
Describe general properties of transition elements (d-Block)
Identify important compounds of transition elements
Differentiate between Lanthanoids and Actinoids
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit 4: d and f Block Elements

competency_groups.title: Coordination Compounds
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Werner's theory, nomenclature, isomerism, bonding theories (including metal carbonyls), and applications.

Define important terms pertaining to coordination compounds
Apply IUPAC rules for nomenclature of coordination compounds
Identify different types of isomerism in coordination compounds
Describe bonding theories including Metal Carbonyls

competency_groups.title: Haloalkanes and Haloarenes
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to classification, nomenclature, C-X bond nature, preparation methods, physical properties, chemical reactions, and polyhalogen compounds.
competencies: Classify haloalkanes and haloarenes based on structure
Apply IUPAC nomenclature rules for halides
Analyze the nature of C-X bond in organic halides
Describe methods of preparation for haloalkanes and haloarenes
Predict physical properties and chemical reactions of halogenated compounds

competency_groups.title: Alcohols, Phenols and Ethers
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to classification, nomenclature, functional group structures, specific properties of alcohols/phenols, and ethers.
competencies: Classify alcohols, phenols, and ethers based on structure
Apply IUPAC nomenclature rules for these compounds
Draw structural representations of functional groups
Differentiate properties between alcohols and phenols

competency_groups.title: Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to nomenclature of carbonyl/carboxyl groups, preparation methods, physical properties, chemical reactions, and uses.
competencies: Apply IUPAC rules for naming aldehydes, ketones, and carboxylic acids
Describe the structure of Carbonyl and Carboxyl groups
Outline methods of preparation for these functional group compounds
Compare physical properties and chemical reactions across carbonyls and acids
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit 8: Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids

Describe the structure and function of proteins

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.
